steps.title: Use the definition of k
steps.content: The value $k$ is $f(1)$. A form that shows $k$ as the coefficient should make the exponential factor equal $1$ when $x=1$.

steps.title: Check the shifted form
steps.content:
In the form $f(x)=98(1.4)^{x-1}$, substituting $x=1$ gives:
$$f(1)=98(1.4)^0=98$$The coefficient is therefore the value of $k$. The answer is C.
